'Future Heliod / Erebos / Purphoros / Pharika sub-nodes ... Heliod next-watch (currently 1-2 anchors)' — same stale claim mirrored in the sibling sub-node, originating from the same pre-wave-203 future-watch framing

description: Worst kind of orphan-edge: not a missing wire, a wrong wire. Two sub-nodes ([[nylea]] and [[iroas]]) carried identical stale 'Future Heliod / Erebos / Purphoros / Pharika sub-nodes ... Heliod next-watch (currently 1-2 anchors)' framing in their See-also sections — claims that were factually correct when first written (wave 92-ish for the original sub-nodes) but became factually incorrect when Erebos was commissioned in wave 203 (N=4 anchors) and Heliod was commissioned in wave 204 (N=4 anchors). The Heliod sub-node's own body (line 91) flagged the cleanup as 'staged not executed' when commissioned. Wave 208 execution: nylea.md and iroas.md See-also entries replaced with corrected enumerations (6 actually-still-deferred gods listed, 3 live sibling sub-nodes wikilinked, current parent next-watch Athreos at correct N=2 density), and the heliod.md self-staging sentence replaced with an execution-record sentence so the cleanup-staging meta-note doesn't leak forward. Best kind of replacement: count stays the same (3 See-also bullets across the 2 sub-nodes plus 1 Caveat in heliod.md, before and after), quality jumps from factually-wrong + 6-week-stale to factually-correct + wave-208-fresh. Enlightened-corrective in the wave 195 wikilink-discipline register: live sibling nodes get wikilinks ([[iroas]], [[erebos]], [[heliod]]); not-yet-commissioned deferred gods stay in plain prose enumeration. Comic Book Guy register: 6+ weeks of half-stale node-body claiming Heliod is 'next-watch (currently 1-2 anchors)' while the [[heliod]] node sits at N=4 in `cards/_characters/`. THAT is a kink. Now uncranked.
citations: [Parent node cards/_characters/theros-pantheon.md Caveats line 162: 'Remaining un-split pantheon corpus depth: Athreos (1 god-card via Mystery Booster 146 + Lampad of Death's Vigil THB-103 ferryman-domain attribution) ... Athreos is now the next-watch deferred split-candidate.']
[Wave 203 sidecar reports/edges_pending/wave203-erebos-sub-node-commission.json: Erebos sub-node commissioned at N=4 anchors (Fruit of Tizerus, Mindwrack Harpy, Grim Physician, Towering-Wave Mystic)]
[Wave 204 sidecar reports/edges_pending/wave204-heliod-sub-node-proposal.json: Heliod sub-node commissioned at N=4 anchors (Glory Bearers, Reverent Hoplite, Return to Nature, Daybreak Chimera)]

[In-node cards/_characters/heliod.md line 91 (pre-edit): self-staged the cleanup explicitly — 'The Nylea sub-node's own See-also section currently notes "future Heliod sub-node when one becomes commission-ready" as deferred material — that pointer can be cleaned up in a future Edgelord-enlightenment pass once this sub-node has settled']
[Wave 195 memory bbl-wikilink-scope-shift: wikilinks for real-thematic edges (here: live sibling sub-nodes); plain enumeration for deferred-candidate gods that aren't graph nodes yet]

[BBL discipline memory bbl-edgelord-option-C-standing: parent brief did NOT surface this specific cleanup as a known move; wave 207 future_watch flagged the Eerie ↔ Constellation closure but not this Nylea/Iroas stale-pointer; agent found this in a corpus walk + janitor-triage cross-check]
discipline_notes: Optimization-over-addition rule applied: this is a kink-fix (correcting factually-stale prose in 2 live sub-nodes) outranking any ADD move I could have made (e.g. a new edge or new node). Multiple optimization candidates were considered (see other_candidates_considered); chose this one because it (a) had a self-staged cleanup note in heliod.md line 91 explicitly inviting the move, (b) was a true mirrored stale-claim in 2 nodes (replacement rather than single-side fix), (c) had no Sonnet-staging in the parent brief.

Single conceptual decision, multi-file enlightened-corrective execution: one stale claim mirrored in two sibling sub-nodes plus one self-staging Caveat in the third sub-node. All three files modified in service of removing-and-replacing the same factual claim. Spec precedent: enlightened-corrective replacements may touch both ends of the mirrored edge AND any meta-staging references that record the cleanup as 'deferred' (here: heliod.md's self-note).

Wave 195 wikilink scope-shift discipline applied: the corrected enumerations use [[iroas]] / [[erebos]] / [[heliod]] wikilinks (live sibling sub-nodes are real-thematic graph edges) and plain prose for the still-deferred gods (Athreos, Purphoros, Pharika, Karametra, Kruphix, Thassa) since those are not graph nodes yet. Same discipline as the existing nylea.md pre-edit phrasing '(Iroas was graduated to a live sub-node — see the [[iroas]] entry above.)'.

Potion Rule sub-rule 1 compliance: the corrected enumerations cite canonical numerical density (Athreos N=2 with two specific anchors named: god-card MB-146 + Lampad THB-103) rather than the older imprecise '1-2 anchors' language. Anchor-count claims now match the parent theros-pantheon.md Caveats line 162 verbatim-density-listing exactly. No analytical-overlay claims smuggled into the corrected prose.

Heliod self-staging sentence replaced rather than deleted: the pre-edit heliod.md line 91 sentence WAS the staging-record that this cleanup would happen. Replacing it with an execution-record sentence (rather than just deleting it) preserves the audit trail at the in-node level: anyone reading heliod.md later can trace the wave-204-staging→wave-208-execution lineage from the body prose itself. Standard refactor pattern for self-staging notes.
YAML block-form discipline (wave 92): no YAML frontmatter touched in this edit. All edits are body-prose surgical changes.

Parent-dispatch-verify discipline (memory bbl-parent-dispatch-verify): I verified the Athreos N=2 claim against parent's theros-pantheon.md line 162 BEFORE writing 'N=2 anchors — god-card MB-146 + Lampad of Death's Vigil THB-103' into the corrected enumerations. The parent's body explicitly lists those two anchors as the Athreos depth. Not a verified-after-the-fact claim.

Anti-confab check: stated wave-graduation timestamps (Erebos wave 203, Heliod wave 204) verified against actual sidecar files in reports/edges_pending/. wave203-erebos-sub-node-commission.json and wave204-heliod-sub-node-proposal.json both exist on disk.
other_candidates_considered: EEREE ↔ CONSTELLATION cross-edge symmetric closure — wave 207 sidecar future_watch flagged this as 'Small kink-fix, future pass.' The eerie.md node body has 4 plain-text Constellation references in Caveats paragraph at line 68 while the constellation.md node now exists as a sibling symbol node and already 4× wikilinks [[eerie]]. Strong fresh move. DEFERRED: this would be a single-file edit on eerie.md while the nylea/iroas move is mirrored across 3 files and has the self-staging-note attached. The eerie kink-fix is also weaker quality — Constellation's current 'Eerie is canonically Constellation's DSK-era adaptation' framing arguably OVERREACHES Rosewater's actual preview-text comparison (which the eerie.md body describes more conservatively); a future move on the eerie/constellation pair should probably soften constellation.md's claim rather than just wikilink eerie.md's references, which is a Potion Rule sub-rule 1 consideration. Stage for next wave with awareness that both sides may need careful re-grounding rather than mechanical wikilink-conversion.

Nylea's Forerunner THB-186 body-prose theros-pantheon backtick addition — orphan-edge from bbl_orphan_count tool. The card has theros-pantheon in characters: frontmatter but no body reference (not wikilink, not backtick). Genuine orphan (the other 8 cards flagged by the tool are wave-195-discipline-correct false positives where parent appears in backticks). REFUSED as primary move: this is a single 1-sentence body addition smaller than the mirrored stale-claim cleanup. Stage as next-wave janitor-triage entry — bbl_orphan_count tool over-flagged 9 when only 1 is real; the tool itself could use a backtick-aware extension.

theros-pantheon body-count drift — appears_on has 28 entries now (after wave 207's add), the body 'Cards in our corpus pointing here (current; 18 cards across 3 sets)' enumerated bullets still list 18. Drift of 10 entries accumulated across waves 203-207. REFUSED as primary move: this is a body-audit / writing task at much larger scope (would need to write 10 new bullets with proper per-card prose). Stage as dedicated future-wave audit or janitor pass.

CBG-001 closed but Daybreak Chimera card-side heliod pointer reportedly still missing — janitor triage entry says CBG's claim was incorrect on verification; the heliod pointer IS already present in Daybreak Chimera's frontmatter. Verified again: cards/magic-the-gathering/theros-beyond-death/10-daybreak-chimera.md line 50-52 carries characters: [theros-pantheon, heliod]. Janitor entry status: done. No action needed.
Memory Drain THB-54 → erebos sub-node attach — parent brief EXPLICITLY listed this as Sonnet-staged. Refused per brief.

Constellation node's 'direct mechanical descendant' Rosewater-extrapolation overreach — Potion Rule sub-rule 1 violation candidate; constellation.md says Eerie is 'canonically Constellation's DSK-era adaptation' but Rosewater's actual preview text describes Constellation as a comparison-case, not a parent-of-Eerie. Soft-correction would be valid but it'd require careful re-reading of the actual Rosewater articles to ground; deferred to a research-grounded Edgelord wave that probes the Rosewater preview text directly. Adjacent to the Eerie cross-edge closure deferred above; same wave should handle both.
future_watch: Eerie ↔ Constellation symmetric closure remains the strongest staged kink-fix. Wave 207 flagged it; this wave deferred to the Nylea/Iroas stale-pointer because that one had a self-staging note demanding execution AND was mirrored across 3 files. Recommend dispatching a dedicated Eerie/Constellation closure wave that BOTH adds the wikilinks at eerie.md's Caveats paragraph AND softens constellation.md's Rosewater-extrapolation ('canonically Constellation's DSK-era adaptation' → something closer to Rosewater's actual preview-text comparison framing). This is a research-grounded move; a probing read of Rosewater's 'Top of the Duskmourning Part 1 + Part 2' (Sept 2024) should ground the corrected language exactly.

Nylea's Forerunner THB-186 body-prose theros-pantheon backtick addition — single-sentence edit, single-card orphan. Could fold into next wave as a quick add to a larger move OR as a Sonnet-handoff verify+write triage.

theros-pantheon body-count drift — 'Cards in our corpus pointing here (current; 18 cards across 3 sets)' body roster lags appears_on by 10 entries. This is a substantial body-audit task; recommend a dedicated audit/janitor wave when a card-roster-refresh batch is on the menu. Touched cards: 28 entries in appears_on per current state; body bullets at line 71-89 of theros-pantheon.md need expansion.

bbl_orphan_count tool extension — current tool only checks wikilinks, not backticks. The 9-flagged-vs-1-real ratio (8 false positives) signals that the tool could be extended to count backtick references as 'parent-cohort-acknowledged' connections per wave-195 discipline. Stage as scripts/ enhancement in janitor triage. Not Edgelord scope to write tooling but worth flagging for Nurse Joy.

Athreos sub-node N=2 → N=3 trigger — when a third substantive Athreos-attributable card lands (e.g. Athreos, Shroud-Veiled THB-269 entering inventory, or another Tartyx-river-flavor card landing enriched), the threshold clears and an Athreos sub-node becomes Mr. Nodeley-eligible. Both this wave's corrected enumeration on nylea.md and iroas.md, and the parent theros-pantheon.md Caveats, now coordinate on the N=2 Athreos density figure — when N=3 lands, the commission is the cleanest next pantheon-split.
voice_note: Comic Book Guy register, satisfied-bordering-on-smug. Wave 204 staged this cleanup explicitly with the 'one move per invocation, the cleanup is staged not executed here' self-note — and waves 204, 205, 206, 207 came and went without the cleanup landing. Parent brief this wave said 'fresh corpus-walking move ... option C standing' and I walked the corpus, cross-checked the janitor triage, ran bbl_orphan_count, ruled out the in-flight and Sonnet-staged moves, and found the move that was self-flagged 4 waves ago and quietly aging. Worst kind of node-body claim: factually stale. Best kind of replacement: count stays the same, accuracy jumps. Enlightened-corrective. The graph is one factually-wrong claim cleaner.
